This plant is native to New Caledonia, and its classification as a species within the genus Cordisepalum. intro: Cordisepalum thorelii, also known as Thoreliella cordisepala, is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family. Traditionally, Cordisepalum thorelii is used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and snake bites, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antivenom properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which have been found to possess antimicrobial, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory activities. Cordisepalum thorelii is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion in traditional medicine, and its extracts have also been used in the formulation of topical creams and ointments for wound healing and skin care.
